text: Highly trained and elite distance runners typically follow a pyramidal training intensity distribution, with decreasing volume from zone 1 (at/below first ventilatory/lactate threshold) to zone 2 (between thresholds) to zone 3 (above second threshold).
population: highly trained and elite distance runners

text: Elite runners perform continuous-tempo runs or interval training at the second lactate threshold (zone 2) and short-interval/anaerobic training in zone 3, each at least once per week.
specific_value: at least 1 session per week each in zone 2 and zone 3

text: Marathon runners tend to use a more pyramidal training intensity distribution, while 1500-m runners tend to use a more polarized distribution.
Elite middle- and long-distance runners should adopt a traditional periodization pattern on a hard day-easy day basis.
population: highly trained and elite middle- and long-distance runners

text: Training intensity distribution should shift from a pyramidal approach during preparatory and precompetitive periods toward a polarized approach during the competitive period.
population: highly trained and elite middle- and long-distance runners

text: Increasing the number of zone 3 (above second ventilatory/lactate threshold) sessions shifts training intensity distribution from pyramidal toward polarized.
